currentState: Chapter 12 cools the seduction into hierarchy. The peer-recognition remains true, but once Kel'Thuzad takes the room Jandice becomes a threshold function and Jaina becomes the scholar he actually wants.
powerDynamic: Jandice still wants recognition from Jaina, but chapter 12 proves that the exchange has already passed beyond her control. Jaina takes the access Jandice opened and moves into the deeper, more dangerous mirror beyond it.

physicalDynamics.pattern: They read each other at the same speed: hands, scars, notation, posture, all of it registering as peer before either woman admits the danger in that resemblance.
Jandice's hands start sketching theory before she catches herself; Jaina's stillness turns that loss of discipline into leverage.

nameUsage.jainaCallsJandice: Starts formal and shifts quickly into direct peer address once the work becomes the subject.
nameUsage.jandiceCallsJaina: Begins with 'Lady Proudmoore' and then slides into a faster, more intimate academic register as recognition takes hold.

history.event: Jandice replaces Alexei as Jaina's guide and immediately turns the encounter from political scrutiny into a controlled scholarly courtship.
The research log and the resonance exchange prove that each woman is exactly the kind of mind the other can neither dismiss nor safely trust.
Chapter 12 gives them only a final threshold beat: Jandice tries to present Jaina's quickness upward, then leaves at once when Kel'Thuzad dismisses her.

trajectory: New in chapter 10: mirror recognition -> tactical intellectual seduction -> controlled descent -> chapter 12's proof that Jandice was the door, not the final room.
